title: Easy Going Vietnamese Tofu Pho
description: A funky and silky Vietnamese dish featuring sushi rice and tofu, perfect for a sick day, and jain-friendly. This recipe is easy to make and delicious to eat.

ingredients.name: firm tofu
ingredients.notes: drained and cut into small cubes

instructions: In a large pot, combine the vegetable broth, sliced onion, sliced ginger, star anise, and cinnamon stick, and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together.
While the broth is simmering, cook the sushi rice according to the package instructions.
In a separate pan, heat 1 tablespoon of oil over medium heat and add the cubed tofu, cooking until golden brown on all sides.
Add the cooked tofu to the simmering broth and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Stir in the soy sauce and lime juice, and season with salt to taste.
To serve, place some cooked sushi rice into a bowl, ladle the hot broth and tofu over the top, and garnish with sliced green onions and a sprinkle of grated ginger.
Refrigerate or freeze any leftovers for up to 3 days.
Reheat the soup over low heat, adding more vegetable broth if needed to achieve the desired consistency.
